section_title: ICT PRICE ACTION MODEL 4: POSITION TRADING TRADE PLAN OVERVIEW
content:
Welcome to ICT Price Action Model Number Four: Position Trading Trade Plan, targeting 500+ Pips per
trade.
This model follows the standard five stages of trade planning:
- Preparation
- Opportunity Discovery
- Trade Planning
- Trade Execution
- Trade Management
Preparation involves referring to the economic calendar for the coming week, noting all medium and high
impact events for the markets you follow. Study these events and consider how current market structure
and calendar events may suggest a specific weekly profile for that week's range. For position trading, the
primary focus is the monthly chart, looking for seasonal tendencies. Economic drivers and volatility
injections from the calendar may act as a 'smoke screen' to facilitate manipulation, initiating longer-term
trades. All components from previous trade plans (swing, short-term, intraday) can be integrated into higher
timeframe trade plans for managing risk, but the core protocols and procedures remain consistent across
models.

For trade preparation, we refer to the economic calendar for the coming week, noting all medium and high
impact events. Study these events to consider how current market structure and calendar events may
suggest a specific weekly profile for that week's range. Since we are focusing on position trading, we
primarily look at the monthly chart and seasonal tendencies. We consider how independent daily economic
drivers and volatility injections from the economic calendar might facilitate the manipulation that starts
these longer-term trades.
The next stage of preparation involves determining the ITA data range. We identify the highest high and
lowest low within the last 20, 40, and 60 trading days (Sundays are not counted). This defines your current
dealing range. The 60-day ITA data range is the furthest we go back, and we look for setups within this
range. The highest high and lowest low from this range will be referred to for our PD Matrix.

Inside this dealing range, we will look for the next draw on liquidity where price is likely to trade next. This
could be an old high/low, a fair value gap, or a liquidity pool. The focus is a minimum of 500 Pips. Many
times, you will find yourself reaching for that 60-day ITA data range to get that 500 Pips.
We will look for a PD array in the direction of the monthly range bias:
- Premium PD arrays when bullish
- Discount arrays when bearish
We anticipate price to move to a PD array that supports our monthly bias on a key economic event found
on the economic calendar within the current or next trading week. This is what we wait for. This can either
be a run on liquidity or rebalancing to inefficient price delivery.

section_title: SEASONAL TENDENCIES AND QUARTERLY SHIFT MODEL
content:
We identify the current seasonal tendencies for the time of your price action study. Refer to the core
content for teachings on commodity markets, seasonal tendencies in Month 5, and the Mega Trades
section where my personal choice of the best seasonal tendencies throughout the year are outlined. For
this long-term position model, you need to:
- Go through all markets and look for their strongest seasonal tendencies throughout the calendar year.
- Plan each month during Christmas break or the first couple of weeks of January, anticipating which
market might move (not demanding it has to happen). Seasonal tendencies are a roadmap, not a Panacea;
they are not always perfect or accurate.
This is truly a quarterly shift model and trade plan, designed to trade for moves that are around two months
or longer, focusing on the quarterly shifts. Limit your focus to only the highest probability seasonals that
suggest a strong chance of bullishness or bearishness at that current time, capable of offering 500 Pips or
more. This means looking at monthly and weekly charts to frame out that 60-day ITA data range, limiting
your focus to the highest probability seasonal tendencies.

section_title: COT HEDGE PROGRAM, PD ARRAYS, AND MARKET PRIMING
content:
We identify discount arrays under the ITA data range of 20, 40, and 60 days when the bias is bearish. You
will identify premium arrays inside the 20, 40, and 60-day ITA data range when the COT (Commitment of
Traders) hedge program is bearish, expecting a monthly range expansion down.
Conversely, identify premium arrays above the 20, 40, and 60-day ITA data range when the bias is bullish.
Identify the discount arrays inside the 20, 40, and 60-day ITA data range when the COT hedge program is
bullish, expecting a monthly range expansion up.
When the market is primed, we look for a convergence of both manipulation and price opposite to our trade
bias, at a time when the economic calendar suggests a volatility injection will likely unfold. We will short
premium fair value gaps and buy discount fair value gap setups.

When we are bearish, we will frame a short entry when price has moved up into a 4-Hour premium fair
value gap PD array that converges with a standard deviation of no more than +3 standard deviation during
London or New York open. We can implement scalping protocols at this stage for further risk reduction,
using the scalping trade plans to refine risk down to the smallest possible on a one-minute chart.
When we are bullish, we will frame a long entry when price has moved down into a 4-Hour discount fair
value gap PD array that converges with a standard deviation of no more than -3 standard deviation during
London open or New York open. Again, scalping protocols can be implemented at this stage for further risk
reduction.

When we are bearish, we will target the sell-side liquidity below any old daily or weekly low or discount fair
value gap inside of the 20, 40, or 60-day ITA data range. The next logical discount array at 500 Pips will be
the initial objective. There will likely be multiple daily or weekly lows inside of the ITA data range, but we
use the one that frames the potential of at least 500 Pips.
When we are bullish, we will target the bullish liquidity above any old daily or weekly high or premium fair
value gap inside of the 20, 40, or 60-day ITA data range. The next logical premium array at 500 Pips will be
the initial objective. There will likely be multiple old daily or weekly highs inside of the ITA data range, but
we use the one that frames the potential of at least 500 Pips.

section_title: WEEKLY PROFILE & INSTITUTIONAL ORDER FLOW ENTRY
content:
When we are bullish, we will note the European open price on Tuesday (which could also be Monday or
Wednesday, focusing on the weekly profile for the low to form between Monday and Wednesday's New
York open). There is a 70% chance that Tuesday creates a low of the week when the market is bullish.
Filter all longs at or below this European open price level, which must overlap with the 4-hour discount fair
value gap.
We will anticipate a 15-minute chart institutional order flow entry drill trade entry to form inside of a
retracement lower during London open and/or New York open Kill Zones, or a sell stop raid.
When we are bearish, we will note the European opening price on Monday, Tuesday, or Wednesday and
filter all shorts at or above this price level, which must overlap in the 4-hour premium fair value gap. We will
anticipate a 15-minute chart institutional order flow entry drill trade entry to form inside of a retracement
higher during London open and/or New York open Kill Zones, or a standard buy stop raid.

section_title: ENTRY, STOP LOSS, AND PROFIT TAKING PROTOCOLS
content:
When entering a short:
- Place a sell limit order on all positions. Execute with a demo account.
- Use the standard deviation and PD convergence minus 5 Pips as your entry price for the sell limit order. If
multiple orders are used, all use the same entry price.
- Place a limit order to take 100 Pips as the objective on one position.
- Place a second limit order to take 250 Pips as the second objective. Use multiple orders to manage the
trade idea.
- If you capture a 500-pip objective, close 80% of the total trade position size across all orders. Then, see if
you have any more room to run in price.
- Note the premium array and standard deviation convergence we aim to enter at. Place your stop loss
above this high plus 25 Pips. Re-enter if the trade stops out; monitor for a secondary entry. Position trades
may require multiple attempts to secure a solid entry; do not fear this.
When entering a long:
- Place a buy limit order on all positions. Execute with a demo account.
- Use the standard deviation and PD array convergence plus 5 Pips as our entry price for the buy limit
order. If multiple orders are used, all use the same entry price.
- Place a limit order to take 100 Pips as the objective on one position.
- Place a second limit order to take 250 Pips as the second objective. Use multiple orders to manage the
trade idea.
- If you capture a 500-pip objective, close 80% of the trade and see if it has more room to run.
For profit taking, consider setting separate limit orders: one for 100 Pips, a second for 250 Pips, and a third
(if applicable) for 500 Pips. At the 500-pip objective, ensure 80% of the total existing trade position size is
closed across all orders.
